And number three, you can actually train open claw. So you can say to open claw, okay, use for example like glm5 turbo, which is another fast API and use GPT 5.4 mini or nano for the like smaller tasks, right? So every time it's using it, it delegates the smaller tasks and the sub aents to these cheaper, faster b models that are designed for repetitive boring tasks. And then you can have a more powerful brain so that you use less tokens for the brain and you use more tokens from the cheaper models for the the heavy lifting, right? The boring repetitive work. So that's basically how this works and and how to to get this working together. Now, if you're wondering, okay, what is GP5 mini and nano in plain English? So forget the complicated names for a second. Basically what's happened is OpenAI makes AR models, right? basically the brain that powers chat GPT. Now, they've just released two brand new, smaller, faster, and cheaper models versions of the most powerful model. So, they're Mini and Nano. Think of it like this. GT5.4 is like a full size sports car. GT5 Mini is like a sports car that's twice as fast, half the size, and does almost the same things for a fraction of the effort. GT 5.4 Nano is like a motorcycle, right? So, it's small, super fast, and built for one job, which is getting things done at massive scale. And these are not like simplified versions. They're just purpose-built for automation speed and running many tasks at the same time. They usually come out after the frontier models come out, right? And mini supports text and image inputs, tool use, web search and function calling. Nano is built for classification, data extraction, sorting, empowering sub agents that all simple fast tasks inside bigger workflows. Both just dropped, right? Mini is available in the API codeex and chat GPT. Nano is API only, meaning it's a developer tool right now for the moment. And here's why you should care if you've never touched an API in your life. Well, these models power the tools you're going to use today. They make everything cheaper and faster and smarter, right? So, when you combine them with OpenClaw, you got something completely different. So, that's basically how it works, how to set them up and that sort of thing. What I'd recommend is something called a dual stack framework. And I'll talk about what that is and how it works in a second. So, you want to think of it in terms of two layers. Layers one is that you've got, for example, the brain. That could be GPT 5.4 itself. It could be clawed but they're basically the AI model that do does the thinking for you and then mini handles or nano handles the fast repetitive tasks right so for example classifying extracting sorting routting and you just pick the right brain for the right job and then you have the hands right so this is open claw the AI agent that implements a plan so the brain thinks the hands act right so openclaw takes the output from the AI model and turns it into real world actions so for example sending emails updating files running workflows posting content browsing the How do they fit together?
